title: Masala Oats

subs.name: a pinch of asafoetida
subs.notes: different aroma but keeps the South Indian character

steps.text: Heat oil in a small saucepan or kadai on medium. Splutter mustard seeds, then add cumin and curry leaves and fry 10 seconds. Add onion and green chilli and sauté 2 minutes until soft.
Add tomato and carrot. Cook stirring for 2 minutes until the tomato collapses. Add turmeric and salt.
Pour in 1 cup water and bring to a vigorous boil. Add the oats and stir well to combine.
Cook on medium heat for 3–4 minutes, stirring constantly, until the oats absorb the water and the mixture thickens to a porridge consistency. It should fall from the spoon in thick ribbons.
Remove from heat, squeeze lemon over, and scatter coriander leaves. Eat immediately — masala oats thicken quickly and are best enjoyed fresh and piping hot.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: oats → broken wheat (dalia), soaked 10 minutes (cook 5 minutes longer; nuttier flavour); carrot → finely diced capsicum or beans (any small-cut vegetable adds texture); curry-leaves → a pinch of asafoetida (different aroma but keeps the South Indian character)
